Class Semisequent

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.sequent.Semisequent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<SequentFormula>
public abstract class Semisequent extends Object implements Iterable<SequentFormula>
Implements a redundancy free list of sequent formulas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
    protected
    Semisequent()
    used by inner class Empty
    protected
    Semisequent(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> seqList)
    Create a new Semisequent from an ordered collection of formulas.

    Method
    Description
    ImmutableList<SequentFormula>
    asList()
    Returns the list of sequent formulas constituting this Semisequent
    boolean
    contains(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
    checks if the SequentFormula occurs in this Semisequent (identity check)
    boolean

    checks if a SequentFormula is in this Semisequent (equality check)

  • Constructor Details

    • Semisequent

      protected Semisequent(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> seqList)
      Create a new Semisequent from an ordered collection of formulas. The provided list must be redundancy free, i.e., the created sequent must be exactly the same as when creating the sequent by subsequently inserting all formulas
      Parameters:
      seqList - list of sequent formulas

    • Semisequent

      protected Semisequent()
      used by inner class Empty

  • Method Details

    • insert

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insert(int idx, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      inserts an element at a specified index performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same Semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      idx - int encoding the place the element has to be put
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new Semisequent and information, which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertFirst

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertFirst(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      inserts element at index 0 performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same Semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks whether this is a Semisequent that contains no formulas
      Returns:
      true if the Semisequent contains no formulas

    • isRedundant

      protected abstract boolean isRedundant(SequentFormula existingFormula, SequentFormula checkedFormula)

    • insertAndRemoveRedundancyHelper

      protected SemisequentChangeInfo insertAndRemoveRedundancyHelper(int idx, SequentFormula sequentFormula, SemisequentChangeInfo semiCI, @Nullable FormulaChangeInfo fci)
      Inserts a new SequentFormula at index idx and removes redundant formulas. This implementation removes only duplicates but the original idea was to realize backward and forward subsumption.
      Parameters:
      idx - the int specifying where to place the formula (if the formula does not already occur in the corresponding Semisequent
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to be inserted
      semiCI - the SemisequentChangeInfo which describes the current status of the modified Semisequent and which will be updated to reflect the change performed by the execution of this method invocation
      fci - null if the formula to be added is new, otherwise an object telling which formula is replaced with the new formula sequentFormula, and what are the differences between the two formulas
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new Semisequent and information, which formulas have been added or removed

    • createSemisequentChangeInfo

      protected final SemisequentChangeInfo createSemisequentChangeInfo(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> seqList)
      A factory method to provide an entry point to allow subclasses to create more specific change information if necessary. At the moment final (if flexibility needed, remove the final modifier)
      Parameters:
      seqList - the list of the formulas considered to be the starting point from which on changes are tracked
      Returns:
      a fresh change information object

    • get

      public SequentFormula get(int idx)
      Retrieves the element at a specific index
      Parameters:
      idx - int representing the index of the element we want to have
      Returns:
      SequentFormula found at index idx
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if idx is negative or greater or equal to size()

    • getFirst

      public SequentFormula getFirst()
      Returns:
      the first SequentFormula of this Semisequent

    • iterator

      public @NonNull Iterator<SequentFormula> iterator()
      Returns iterator about the formulas contained in this Semisequent
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<SequentFormula>
      Returns:
      iterator about the formulas contained in this Semisequent

    • asList

      public ImmutableList<SequentFormula> asList()
      Returns the list of sequent formulas constituting this Semisequent
      Returns:
      an ImmutableList of SequentFormulas contained in this Semisequent

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of formulas of this Semisequent
      Returns:
      number of formulas of this Semisequent

    • equals

      public boolean equals(@Nullable Object other)
      Two Semisequents are equal if they contain the same formulas in the same order.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      other - the Object to which this Semisequent is compared
      Returns:
      true if and only if the other Semisequent contains the same formulas in the same order.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      String representation of this Semisequent

    • insert

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insert(int idx, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions)
      Inserts the elements of the list at the specified index performing redundancy checks
      Parameters:
      idx - int encoding the place where the insertion starts
      insertions - the ImmutableList of SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new Semisequent and information, which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertLast

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertLast(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      Inserts element at the end of the semisequent performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • indexOf

      public int indexOf(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      Returns the index of the given SequentFormula or -1 if the sequent formula is not found. Equality of sequent formulas is checked sing the identity check (i.e., ==)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      index of sequentFormula (-1 if not found)

    • insertFirst

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertFirst(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions)
      Inserts element at index 0 performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same Semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      insertions - the ImmutableList of SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new Semisequent and information, which formulas have been added or removed

    • insertLast

      public SemisequentChangeInfo insertLast(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions)
      inserts the formulas of the list at the end of the semisequent performing redundancy checks, this may result in returning same semisequent if inserting would create redundancies
      Parameters:
      insertions - the ImmutableList of SequentFormula to be inserted
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new Semisequent and information, which formulas have been added or removed

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(PosInOccurrence pos, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements)
      replaces the element at place idx with the first element of the given list and adds the rest of the list to the semisequent behind the replaced formula
      Parameters:
      pos - the formula to be replaced
      replacements - the IList whose head replaces the element at index idx and the tail is added to the semisequent
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • remove

      public SemisequentChangeInfo remove(int idx)
      removes an element
      Parameters:
      idx - int being the index of the element that has to be removed
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(PosInOccurrence pos, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      replaces the element at place idx with sequentFormula
      Parameters:
      pos - the PosInOccurrence describing the position of and within the formula below which the formula differs from the new formula sequentFormula
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula replacing the old element at index idx
      Returns:
      a semi sequent change information object with the new semisequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(int idx, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      replaces the element at place idx with sequentFormula
      Parameters:
      idx - an int specifying the index of the element that has to be replaced
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula replacing the old element at index idx
      Returns:
      semisequent change information object with new semisequent with sequentFormula at place idx

    • replace

      public SemisequentChangeInfo replace(int idx, ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements)
      replaces the formula at position idx by the given list of formulas
      Parameters:
      idx - the position
      replacements - the new formulas
      Returns:
      change information including the resulting semisequent after the replacement

    • contains

      public boolean contains(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      checks if the SequentFormula occurs in this Semisequent (identity check)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      true iff. sequentFormula has been found in this Semisequent

    • containsEqual

      public boolean containsEqual(SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      checks if a SequentFormula is in this Semisequent (equality check)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to look for
      Returns:
      true iff. sequentFormula has been found in this Semisequent
